1→# Founder OS Installer & Distribution Design Spec
     2→
     3→**Date:** 2026-03-11
     4→**Status:** Approved
     5→**Scope:** One-command installer for the full Founder OS ecosystem
     6→
     7→## Problem
     8→
     9→Installing Founder OS requires ~11 manual steps across 4 systems (Notion, Google, Node.js, Claude Code). Users must configure API keys, install MCP servers, set up 32 plugins individually, create Notion databases, and authenticate with Google — all without a unified guide. This friction blocks adoption.
    10→
    11→## Solution
    12→
    13→A Claude Code marketplace + plugin structure that lets users install with two commands (`claude plugin marketplace add` + `claude plugin install`). No custom scripts needed. Combined with a `/founder-os:setup:notion-hq` command that programmatically creates all 22 Notion HQ databases.
    14→
    15→## Target User
    16→
    17→Technical founders comfortable with terminal, environment variables, and CLI tools. They want speed and reliability, not hand-holding.
    18→
    19→## Distribution Model
    20→
    21→**Marketplace + plugin pattern.** The distribution repo (`github.com/thecloudtips/founder-os`) serves as both a Claude Code marketplace and a plugin container. Root `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` declares the marketplace; `plugin/` subdirectory contains the actual plugin content. Users install via standard Claude Code plugin commands — no custom scripts needed.
    22→
    23→### Dev Repo (this repo)
    24→Contains everything — plugin source, infrastructure, specs, plans, templates, social content, beads workflow. Not public-facing.
    25→
    26→### Distribution Repo (`github.com/thecloudtips/founder-os`)
    27→Clean, public repo structured as a Claude Code marketplace. Users add the marketplace and install the plugin with two commands.
    28→
    29→### What Ships vs What Stays
    30→
    31→| Ships to dist repo | Stays in dev repo only |
    32→|--------------------|----------------------|
    33→| `plugin/commands/` (all 32 namespaces) | `docs/superpowers/` (specs & plans) |
    34→| `plugin/skills/` | `docs/plans/` (design decisions) |
    35→| `plugin/agents/` | `docs/reports/`, `docs/reference/` |
    36→| `plugin/_infrastructure/` (excl. `deprecated/`) | `docs/agent-specs/` |
    37→| `plugin/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` | `_templates/` (dev scaffolding) |
    38→| `plugin/.mcp.json` (auto-configures Notion + Filesystem) | `social/` (blog & marketing) |
    39→| `plugin/CLAUDE.md` (user-facing) | `.beads/`, `.swarm/` (dev workflow) |
    40→| `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` | `_infrastructure/mcp-configs/deprecated/` |
    41→| `docs/getting-started/` | `CLAUDE.md` (dev version, this file) |
    42→| `CLAUDE.md` (root, marketplace context) | |
    43→| `README.md` (user-facing) | |
    44→
    45→### Release Script (`scripts/release.sh`)
    46→
    47→A bash script in the dev repo that:
    48→1. Validates the distribution repo target directory exists
    49→2. Syncs plugin content into `plugin/` subdirectory via `rsync --delete`
    50→3. Generates `plugin/.mcp.json` for automatic MCP server configuration
    51→4. Generates `plugin/CLAUDE.md` (user-facing, stripped of dev workflow)
    52→5. Preserves marketplace.json at dist repo root (NOT synced from dev)
    53→6. Commits and optionally tags a version in the dist repo
    54→
    55→```bash
    56→# Usage from dev repo root:
    57→./scripts/release.sh [--tag v1.0.0] [--push]
    58→```
    59→
    60→### User-Facing CLAUDE.md
    61→
    62→The plugin directory gets a simplified `CLAUDE.md` containing:
    63→- Project overview (what Founder OS is)
    64→- Plugin quick reference table
    65→- Plugin dependencies
    66→- Conventions (naming, patterns, HQ DB discovery)
    67→- Notion HQ structure
    68→- No references to beads, specs, plans, templates, or dev workflow
    69→
    70→### Distribution Repo Structure
    71→
    72→```
    73→founder-os/                              # Marketplace root
    74→├── .claude-plugin/
    75→│   └── marketplace.json                 # Declares this repo as a marketplace
    76→├── plugin/                              # The actual plugin
    77→│   ├── .claude-plugin/
    78→│   │   └── plugin.json                  # Plugin manifest
    79→│   ├── .mcp.json                        # Auto-configures Notion + Filesystem MCP
    80→│   ├── commands/                        # All 32 namespaces
    81→│   ├── skills/                          # Domain knowledge per namespace
    82→│   ├── agents/                          # Agent teams (7 namespaces)
    83→│   ├── _infrastructure/                 # Shared skills, templates, configs
    84→│   └── CLAUDE.md                        # User-facing plugin documentation
    85→├── docs/
    86→│   └── getting-started/
    87→│       ├── SETUP-GUIDE.md
    88→│       ├── FAQ.md
    89→│       └── TROUBLESHOOTING.md
    90→├── CLAUDE.md                            # Root marketplace context
    91→└── README.md
    92→```
    93→
    94→## Install Flow (User Perspective)
    95→
    96→```bash
    97→# Add marketplace and install plugin (2 commands)
    98→claude plugin marketplace add thecloudtips/founder-os
    99→claude plugin install founder-os
   100→
   101→# Set Notion API key
   102→export NOTION_API_KEY=ntn_your_token_here
   103→
   104→# Optional: set up Notion HQ databases (inside Claude Code)
   105→/founder-os:setup:notion-hq
   106→```
   107→
   108→## New Files
   109→
   110→| File | Purpose | Ships to dist? |
   111→|------|---------|----------------|
   112→| `install.sh` | Main installer script (~300-400 lines bash) | Yes |
   113→| `.env.example` | Template with all config vars + comments | Yes |
   114→| `.gitignore` | Secrets, OS junk, generated files protection | Yes (dist version) |
   115→| `.mcp.json.example` | Template MCP config (reference, installer generates real one) | Yes |
   116→| `docs/getting-started/SETUP-GUIDE.md` | Full walkthrough with API key instructions | Yes |
   117→| `docs/getting-started/FAQ.md` | ~15-20 common questions | Yes |
   118→| `docs/getting-started/TROUBLESHOOTING.md` | Symptom/Cause/Fix/Verify format | Yes |
   119→| `founder-os-setup/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` | Setup plugin manifest | Yes |
   120→| `founder-os-setup/skills/notion-hq-setup/SKILL.md` | Notion HQ deployment logic | Yes |
   121→| `founder-os-setup/commands/setup-notion-hq.md` | `/setup:notion-hq` slash command | Yes |
   122→| `founder-os-setup/commands/setup-verify.md` | `/setup:verify` slash command | Yes |
   123→| `scripts/release.sh` | Syncs user-facing content to dist repo | No (dev only) |
   124→| `CLAUDE.md` (user-facing) | Simplified project guide for end users | Yes (generated) |
   125→| `README.md` | Public-facing project README | Yes |
   126→
   127→## Installer Script Design (install.sh)
   128→
   129→### Phase 1 — Prerequisites Check
   130→
   131→- Verify Node.js >= 18 (`node --version`)
   132→- Verify `npx` available
   133→- Verify Claude Code installed (`claude --version`)
   134→- Verify `gws` CLI installed (`gws --version`)
   135→- Print clear install instructions for anything missing, then exit
   136→
   137→### Phase 2 — Environment Setup
   138→
   139→- Check for `.env` file; if missing, copy `.env.example` and prompt user to fill it
   140→- If `.env` exists but `NOTION_API_KEY` is still the placeholder value, fall back to interactive prompt
   141→- Validate `NOTION_API_KEY` by hitting Notion API (`curl` to list databases)
   142→- Create `WORKSPACE_DIR` if it doesn't exist
   143→- Optionally validate `SLACK_BOT_TOKEN` if provided
   144→
   145→### Phase 3 — Google Authentication
   146→
   147→- Check if `gws auth` is already authenticated (`gws auth status` or similar)
   148→- If not, run `gws auth login` interactively (opens browser)
   149→- Validate with a quick `gws gmail list --limit=1`
   150→
   151→### Phase 4 — Plugin Installation
   152→
   153→- Create `.claude/plugins/` in project root if not exists
   154→- Symlink all 32 `founder-os-*/` directories into `.claude/plugins/`
   155→- **Merge** Notion + Filesystem server entries into existing project-level `.mcp.json` (preserves any existing entries like `claude-flow`). Read existing file first, add new keys, write back. Never overwrite.
   156→- Fix P01 Inbox Zero missing `.mcp.json` (known gotcha)
   157→
   158→### Phase 5 — Notion HQ Setup (Automated)
   159→
   160→**Execution mechanism:** The bash script invokes `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions -p "Run /setup:notion-hq"` which triggers the setup plugin's slash command inside Claude Code. This allows the skill to use Notion MCP tools natively. The script waits for the command to complete and checks the exit code.
   161→
   162→The `/setup:notion-hq` skill:
   163→- Reads `_infrastructure/notion-db-templates/founder-os-hq-manifest.json` for the full DB map (22 databases in `creation_order`)
   164→- Reads each database template JSON by filename from the manifest (mix of `hq-*.json` and legacy-named templates like `crm-sync-hub-communications.json`)
   165→- Uses Notion MCP tools to:
   166→  1. Create a top-level "Founder OS HQ" page as the workspace root
   167→  2. Create all 22 databases under it with correct properties and schema
   168→  3. Wire up cross-database relations (Companies as hub)
   169→  4. Create the Command Center dashboard page with linked views
   170→  5. Populate seed data (status options, type selects, etc.)
   171→- Validates all databases listed in the manifest exist and have correct properties
   172→- **Idempotent / partial recovery:** Before creating any database, searches for existing `[FOS] [Name]`. If found, skips. If 12 of 22 exist from a previous partial run, creates only the missing 10.
   173→
   174→**Fallback:** If `claude` CLI invocation fails, prints manual instructions: "Open Claude Code in this project directory and run `/setup:notion-hq`"
   175→
   176→### Phase 6 — Verification
   177→
   178→- Test Notion connectivity (list all HQ databases)
   179→- Test gws connectivity (Gmail, Calendar, Drive — quick read-only calls)
   180→- Test filesystem MCP (write/read a temp file in `WORKSPACE_DIR`)
   181→- Print summary: green checkmarks for working, red X for failures with links to troubleshooting doc
   182→
   183→### Flags
   184→
   185→- `./install.sh` — full run (skips completed phases automatically)
   186→- `./install.sh --verify` — only run Phase 6
   187→- `./install.sh --skip-notion` — skip Phase 5 (for users with existing Notion setup or no Notion)
   188→- `./install.sh --reset` — remove symlinks and generated MCP entries for clean reinstall
   189→
   190→### .env Loading
   191→
   192→The script loads `.env` using safe parsing that handles quoted values and special characters:
   193→```bash
   194→set -a
   195→source .env
   196→set +a
   197→```
   198→Values with spaces or special characters must be quoted in `.env` (documented in `.env.example`).
   199→
   200→## .env.example
   201→
   202→```bash
   203→# ============================================
   204→# Founder OS Configuration
   205→# ============================================
   206→# Copy this file to .env and fill in your values:
   207→#   cp .env.example .env
   208→#
   209→# See docs/getting-started/SETUP-GUIDE.md for
   210→# step-by-step instructions on getting each key.
   211→# ============================================
   212→
   213→# --- REQUIRED ---
   214→
   215→# Notion Integration Token
   216→# Get yours at: https://www.notion.so/my-integrations
   217→# Create integration → Copy "Internal Integration Secret"
   218→# Starts with: ntn_
   219→NOTION_API_KEY=ntn_your_token_here
   220→
   221→# Workspace directory for file operations (reports, exports, etc.)
   222→# This directory will be created if it doesn't exist
   223→WORKSPACE_DIR=~/founder-os-workspace
   224→
   225→# --- OPTIONAL ---
   226→
   227→# Slack Bot Token (for P19 Slack Digest)
   228→# Get yours at: https://api.slack.com/apps → Create App → Bot Token
   229→# Starts with: xoxb-
   230→SLACK_BOT_TOKEN=
   231→
   232→# Web Search API Key (for P08 Newsletter Engine, P15 Competitive Intel)
   233→WEB_SEARCH_API_KEY=
   234→```
   235→
   236→## .gitignore
   237→
   238→The installer **appends** to any existing `.gitignore` rather than overwriting. It checks for each entry before adding to avoid duplicates.
   239→
   240→```gitignore
   241→# Secrets & credentials
   242→.env
   243→.env.local
   244→.env.*.local
   245→credentials.json
   246→token.json
   247→*.pem
   248→*.key
   249→*.p12
   250→*.pfx
   251→service-account.json
   252→
   253→# Local plugin symlinks (generated by installer)
   254→.claude/plugins/
   255→
   256→# Node
   257→node_modules/
   258→
   259→# OS files
   260→.DS_Store
   261→Thumbs.db
   262→Desktop.ini
   263→
   264→# IDE
   265→.vscode/
   266→.idea/
   267→*.swp
   268→*.swo
   269→*~
   270→
   271→# Logs & temp
   272→*.log
   273→tmp/
   274→.tmp/
   275→
   276→# gws auth cache
   277→.gws/
   278→
   279→# Build artifacts
   280→dist/
   281→build/
   282→```
   283→
   284→Key decisions:
   285→- `.mcp.json` stays tracked in git (installer merges entries into the existing file, which already contains `claude-flow` config). `.mcp.json.example` is committed as reference for what the installer adds.
   286→- `.claude/plugins/` ignored because those are local symlinks
   287→- `.env` never committed; `.env.example` is committed
   288→
   289→## Documentation Design
   290→
   291→### SETUP-GUIDE.md
   292→
   293→Comprehensive walkthrough:
   294→
   295→1. **Overview** — What Founder OS is, what you're installing (32 plugins, 22 Notion databases, MCP integrations)
   296→2. **Prerequisites** — What to install first with links:
   297→   - Claude Code (link to Anthropic docs)
   298→   - Node.js 18+ (link to nodejs.org)
   299→   - `gws` CLI (link to repo + install command)
   300→3. **Getting Your API Keys** — Step-by-step:
   301→   - **Notion API Key**: notion.so/my-integrations → New integration → Name "Founder OS" → Copy "Internal Integration Secret" (starts with `ntn_`) → Must have "Read content", "Update content", "Insert content" capabilities
   302→   - **Google (gws)**: Run `gws auth login` → Browser opens → Sign in → Approve Gmail + Calendar + Drive scopes → Terminal confirms
   303→   - **Slack Bot Token** (optional): api.slack.com/apps → Create New App → From Manifest → Add bot scopes (`channels:history`, `channels:read`, `users:read`) → Install to Workspace → Copy Bot User OAuth Token (starts with `xoxb-`)
   304→   - **Web Search API Key** (optional): Brief instructions for search provider
   305→4. **Installation** — The 3 commands (clone, configure, run)
   306→5. **What the Installer Does** — Transparent breakdown of all 6 phases
   307→6. **First Commands to Try** — 3-4 example commands to validate end-to-end
   308→7. **Updating** — `git pull && ./install.sh` (idempotent)
   309→
   310→### FAQ.md
   311→
   312→~15-20 entries including:
   313→- "Do I need all 32 plugins?" — Yes they all install, use what you need
   314→- "Can I use this without Notion?" — Limited, Notion is backbone for 21 plugins
   315→- "What Google permissions does gws need?" — Read-only by default, write for Calendar/Drive features
   316→- "Is my data sent anywhere?" — No, everything local, API calls direct to Notion/Google
   317→- "Can I add custom plugins?" — Yes, follow plugin scaffold template
   318→- "How do I update?" — `git pull && ./install.sh`
   319→- "Can I use with a team?" — Each person runs installer with own API keys
   320→- "What about older Notion databases?" — Migration guide, backward-compatible discovery
   321→
   322→### TROUBLESHOOTING.md
   323→
   324→Each entry follows Symptom → Cause → Fix → Verify:
   325→
   326→- "Notion API key invalid" — Check starts with `ntn_`, integration has capabilities, page shared
   327→- "gws auth fails" — Browser didn't open, wrong account, missing scopes
   328→- "`npx` command not found" — Node.js not installed/not in PATH
   329→- "Database not found" — Run `./install.sh --verify`, check integration access
   330→- "Permission denied on install.sh" — `chmod +x install.sh`
   331→- "Plugin not showing in Claude Code" — Check symlinks, restart Claude Code
   332→- "MCP server connection failed" — Verify `.mcp.json` env vars, check `NOTION_API_KEY` exported
   333→- "gws rate limited" — Wait 60s, retry, reduce `--limit`
   334→
   335→## Setup Plugin (founder-os-setup)
   336→
   337→A dedicated plugin that provides installation and maintenance commands. Lives alongside the 32 product plugins as plugin #00.
   338→
   339→### Structure
   340→
   341→```
   342→founder-os-setup/
   343→├── .claude-plugin/
   344→│   └── plugin.json           # Manifest (plugin_number: "00", type: "infrastructure")
   345→├── .mcp.json                 # Notion MCP config
   346→├── commands/
   347→│   ├── setup-notion-hq.md    # /setup:notion-hq — create all HQ databases
   348→│   └── setup-verify.md       # /setup:verify — health check all integrations
   349→├── skills/
   350→│   └── notion-hq-setup/
   351→│       └── SKILL.md          # Database creation logic, schema reference
   352→├── INSTALL.md
   353→└── README.md
   354→```
   355→
   356→### `/setup:notion-hq` Command
   357→
   358→Invokes the `notion-hq-setup` skill which:
   359→1. Reads `_infrastructure/notion-db-templates/founder-os-hq-manifest.json` for the complete database map (22 databases in `creation_order`)
   360→2. Reads each template JSON by filename from the manifest (handles both `hq-*.json` and legacy-named templates)
   361→3. Creates databases via Notion MCP in dependency order:
   362→   - Companies first (hub for all relations)
   363→   - Then Contacts, Deals, Communications (CRM cluster)
   364→   - Then Tasks, Meetings, Finance (Operations)
   365→   - Then Briefings, Knowledge Base, Research, Reports (Intelligence)
   366→   - Then Content, Deliverables, Prompts (Content)
   367→   - Then Goals, Milestones, Learnings, Weekly Insights, Workflows, Activity Log (Growth)
   368→4. Wires cross-database relations after all databases exist
   369→5. Creates Command Center dashboard page
   370→6. Populates select/multi-select options (status values, type values)
   371→
   372→### `/setup:verify` Command
   373→
   374→Runs all Phase 6 verification checks from within Claude Code:
   375→- Notion DB connectivity (all 22 databases)
   376→- gws CLI auth status
   377→- Filesystem MCP write/read test
   378→- Reports results with pass/fail per integration
   379→
   380→### Idempotency
   381→
   382→- Before creating any database, searches for existing `[FOS] [Name]` database
   383→- If found, validates schema matches expected properties
   384→- Reports discrepancies but does not overwrite existing data
   385→- Handles partial state: if 12 of 22 databases exist, creates only the missing 10
   386→- Safe to re-run at any time
   387→
   388→## Design Principles
   389→
   390→- **Idempotent** — safe to re-run, skips completed steps
   391→- **Fail-fast** — stops at first blocker with clear error + troubleshooting link
   392→- **No manual Notion setup** — `/setup:notion-hq` creates all 22 databases programmatically
   393→- **Secrets never committed** — `.env` in `.gitignore`, `.env.example` committed
   394→- **Updatable** — `git pull && ./install.sh` handles updates
   395→- **Project-scoped** — plugins symlinked to `.claude/plugins/` (project level, not global)
   396→- **Transparent** — user can read exactly what the installer does before running it
   397→- **Clean separation** — dev workflow artifacts never reach end users; release script enforces the boundary
   398→

file.content:
# Founder OS Installer & Distribution Design Spec

**Date:** 2026-03-11
**Status:** Approved
**Scope:** One-command installer for the full Founder OS ecosystem

## Problem

Installing Founder OS requires ~11 manual steps across 4 systems (Notion, Google, Node.js, Claude Code). Users must configure API keys, install MCP servers, set up 32 plugins individually, create Notion databases, and authenticate with Google — all without a unified guide. This friction blocks adoption.

## Solution

A Claude Code marketplace + plugin structure that lets users install with two commands (`claude plugin marketplace add` + `claude plugin install`). No custom scripts needed. Combined with a `/founder-os:setup:notion-hq` command that programmatically creates all 22 Notion HQ databases.

## Target User

Technical founders comfortable with terminal, environment variables, and CLI tools. They want speed and reliability, not hand-holding.

## Distribution Model

**Marketplace + plugin pattern.** The distribution repo (`github.com/thecloudtips/founder-os`) serves as both a Claude Code marketplace and a plugin container. Root `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` declares the marketplace; `plugin/` subdirectory contains the actual plugin content. Users install via standard Claude Code plugin commands — no custom scripts needed.

### Dev Repo (this repo)
Contains everything — plugin source, infrastructure, specs, plans, templates, social content, beads workflow. Not public-facing.

### Distribution Repo (`github.com/thecloudtips/founder-os`)
Clean, public repo structured as a Claude Code marketplace. Users add the marketplace and install the plugin with two commands.

### What Ships vs What Stays

| Ships to dist repo | Stays in dev repo only |
|--------------------|----------------------|
| `plugin/commands/` (all 32 namespaces) | `docs/superpowers/` (specs & plans) |
| `plugin/skills/` | `docs/plans/` (design decisions) |
| `plugin/agents/` | `docs/reports/`, `docs/reference/` |
| `plugin/_infrastructure/` (excl. `deprecated/`) | `docs/agent-specs/` |
| `plugin/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` | `_templates/` (dev scaffolding) |
| `plugin/.mcp.json` (auto-configures Notion + Filesystem) | `social/` (blog & marketing) |
| `plugin/CLAUDE.md` (user-facing) | `.beads/`, `.swarm/` (dev workflow) |
| `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` | `_infrastructure/mcp-configs/deprecated/` |
| `docs/getting-started/` | `CLAUDE.md` (dev version, this file) |
| `CLAUDE.md` (root, marketplace context) | |
| `README.md` (user-facing) | |

### Release Script (`scripts/release.sh`)

A bash script in the dev repo that:
1. Validates the distribution repo target directory exists
2. Syncs plugin content into `plugin/` subdirectory via `rsync --delete`
3. Generates `plugin/.mcp.json` for automatic MCP server configuration
4. Generates `plugin/CLAUDE.md` (user-facing, stripped of dev workflow)
5. Preserves marketplace.json at dist repo root (NOT synced from dev)
6. Commits and optionally tags a version in the dist repo

```bash
# Usage from dev repo root:
./scripts/release.sh [--tag v1.0.0] [--push]
```

### User-Facing CLAUDE.md

The plugin directory gets a simplified `CLAUDE.md` containing:
- Project overview (what Founder OS is)
- Plugin quick reference table
- Plugin dependencies
- Conventions (naming, patterns, HQ DB discovery)
- Notion HQ structure
- No references to beads, specs, plans, templates, or dev workflow

### Distribution Repo Structure

```
founder-os/                              # Marketplace root
├── .claude-plugin/
│   └── marketplace.json                 # Declares this repo as a marketplace
├── plugin/                              # The actual plugin
│   ├── .claude-plugin/
│   │   └── plugin.json                  # Plugin manifest
│   ├── .mcp.json                        # Auto-configures Notion + Filesystem MCP
│   ├── commands/                        # All 32 namespaces
│   ├── skills/                          # Domain knowledge per namespace
│   ├── agents/                          # Agent teams (7 namespaces)
│   ├── _infrastructure/                 # Shared skills, templates, configs
│   └── CLAUDE.md                        # User-facing plugin documentation
├── docs/
│   └── getting-started/
│       ├── SETUP-GUIDE.md
│       ├── FAQ.md
│       └── TROUBLESHOOTING.md
├── CLAUDE.md                            # Root marketplace context
└── README.md
```

## Install Flow (User Perspective)

```bash
# Add marketplace and install plugin (2 commands)
claude plugin marketplace add thecloudtips/founder-os
claude plugin install founder-os

# Set Notion API key
export NOTION_API_KEY=ntn_your_token_here

# Optional: set up Notion HQ databases (inside Claude Code)
/founder-os:setup:notion-hq
```

## New Files

| File | Purpose | Ships to dist? |
|------|---------|----------------|
| `install.sh` | Main installer script (~300-400 lines bash) | Yes |
| `.env.example` | Template with all config vars + comments | Yes |
| `.gitignore` | Secrets, OS junk, generated files protection | Yes (dist version) |
| `.mcp.json.example` | Template MCP config (reference, installer generates real one) | Yes |
| `docs/getting-started/SETUP-GUIDE.md` | Full walkthrough with API key instructions | Yes |
| `docs/getting-started/FAQ.md` | ~15-20 common questions | Yes |
| `docs/getting-started/TROUBLESHOOTING.md` | Symptom/Cause/Fix/Verify format | Yes |
| `founder-os-setup/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` | Setup plugin manifest | Yes |
| `founder-os-setup/skills/notion-hq-setup/SKILL.md` | Notion HQ deployment logic | Yes |
| `founder-os-setup/commands/setup-notion-hq.md` | `/setup:notion-hq` slash command | Yes |
| `founder-os-setup/commands/setup-verify.md` | `/setup:verify` slash command | Yes |
| `scripts/release.sh` | Syncs user-facing content to dist repo | No (dev only) |
| `CLAUDE.md` (user-facing) | Simplified project guide for end users | Yes (generated) |
| `README.md` | Public-facing project README | Yes |

## Installer Script Design (install.sh)

### Phase 1 — Prerequisites Check

- Verify Node.js >= 18 (`node --version`)
- Verify `npx` available
- Verify Claude Code installed (`claude --version`)
- Verify `gws` CLI installed (`gws --version`)
- Print clear install instructions for anything missing, then exit

### Phase 2 — Environment Setup

- Check for `.env` file; if missing, copy `.env.example` and prompt user to fill it
- If `.env` exists but `NOTION_API_KEY` is still the placeholder value, fall back to interactive prompt
- Validate `NOTION_API_KEY` by hitting Notion API (`curl` to list databases)
- Create `WORKSPACE_DIR` if it doesn't exist
- Optionally validate `SLACK_BOT_TOKEN` if provided

### Phase 3 — Google Authentication

- Check if `gws auth` is already authenticated (`gws auth status` or similar)
- If not, run `gws auth login` interactively (opens browser)
- Validate with a quick `gws gmail list --limit=1`

### Phase 4 — Plugin Installation

- Create `.claude/plugins/` in project root if not exists
- Symlink all 32 `founder-os-*/` directories into `.claude/plugins/`
- **Merge** Notion + Filesystem server entries into existing project-level `.mcp.json` (preserves any existing entries like `claude-flow`). Read existing file first, add new keys, write back. Never overwrite.
- Fix P01 Inbox Zero missing `.mcp.json` (known gotcha)

### Phase 5 — Notion HQ Setup (Automated)

**Execution mechanism:** The bash script invokes `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions -p "Run /setup:notion-hq"` which triggers the setup plugin's slash command inside Claude Code. This allows the skill to use Notion MCP tools natively. The script waits for the command to complete and checks the exit code.

The `/setup:notion-hq` skill:
- Reads `_infrastructure/notion-db-templates/founder-os-hq-manifest.json` for the full DB map (22 databases in `creation_order`)
- Reads each database template JSON by filename from the manifest (mix of `hq-*.json` and legacy-named templates like `crm-sync-hub-communications.json`)
- Uses Notion MCP tools to:
  1. Create a top-level "Founder OS HQ" page as the workspace root
  2. Create all 22 databases under it with correct properties and schema
  3. Wire up cross-database relations (Companies as hub)
  4. Create the Command Center dashboard page with linked views
  5. Populate seed data (status options, type selects, etc.)
- Validates all databases listed in the manifest exist and have correct properties
- **Idempotent / partial recovery:** Before creating any database, searches for existing `[FOS] [Name]`. If found, skips. If 12 of 22 exist from a previous partial run, creates only the missing 10.

**Fallback:** If `claude` CLI invocation fails, prints manual instructions: "Open Claude Code in this project directory and run `/setup:notion-hq`"

### Phase 6 — Verification

- Test Notion connectivity (list all HQ databases)
- Test gws connectivity (Gmail, Calendar, Drive — quick read-only calls)
- Test filesystem MCP (write/read a temp file in `WORKSPACE_DIR`)
- Print summary: green checkmarks for working, red X for failures with links to troubleshooting doc

### Flags

- `./install.sh` — full run (skips completed phases automatically)
- `./install.sh --verify` — only run Phase 6
- `./install.sh --skip-notion` — skip Phase 5 (for users with existing Notion setup or no Notion)
- `./install.sh --reset` — remove symlinks and generated MCP entries for clean reinstall

### .env Loading

The script loads `.env` using safe parsing that handles quoted values and special characters:
```bash
set -a
source .env
set +a
```
Values with spaces or special characters must be quoted in `.env` (documented in `.env.example`).

## .env.example

```bash
# ============================================
# Founder OS Configuration
# ============================================
# Copy this file to .env and fill in your values:
#   cp .env.example .env
#
# See docs/getting-started/SETUP-GUIDE.md for
# step-by-step instructions on getting each key.
# ============================================

# --- REQUIRED ---

# Notion Integration Token
# Get yours at: https://www.notion.so/my-integrations
# Create integration → Copy "Internal Integration Secret"
# Starts with: ntn_
NOTION_API_KEY=ntn_your_token_here

# Workspace directory for file operations (reports, exports, etc.)
# This directory will be created if it doesn't exist
WORKSPACE_DIR=~/founder-os-workspace

# --- OPTIONAL ---

# Slack Bot Token (for P19 Slack Digest)
# Get yours at: https://api.slack.com/apps → Create App → Bot Token
# Starts with: xoxb-
SLACK_BOT_TOKEN=

# Web Search API Key (for P08 Newsletter Engine, P15 Competitive Intel)
WEB_SEARCH_API_KEY=
```

## .gitignore

The installer **appends** to any existing `.gitignore` rather than overwriting. It checks for each entry before adding to avoid duplicates.

```gitignore
# Secrets & credentials
.env
.env.local
.env.*.local
credentials.json
token.json
*.pem
*.key
*.p12
*.pfx
service-account.json

# Local plugin symlinks (generated by installer)
.claude/plugins/

# Node
node_modules/

# OS files
.DS_Store
Thumbs.db
Desktop.ini

# IDE
.vscode/
.idea/
*.swp
*.swo
*~

# Logs & temp
*.log
tmp/
.tmp/

# gws auth cache
.gws/

# Build artifacts
dist/
build/
```

Key decisions:
- `.mcp.json` stays tracked in git (installer merges entries into the existing file, which already contains `claude-flow` config). `.mcp.json.example` is committed as reference for what the installer adds.
- `.claude/plugins/` ignored because those are local symlinks
- `.env` never committed; `.env.example` is committed

## Documentation Design

### SETUP-GUIDE.md

Comprehensive walkthrough:

1. **Overview** — What Founder OS is, what you're installing (32 plugins, 22 Notion databases, MCP integrations)
2. **Prerequisites** — What to install first with links:
   - Claude Code (link to Anthropic docs)
   - Node.js 18+ (link to nodejs.org)
   - `gws` CLI (link to repo + install command)
3. **Getting Your API Keys** — Step-by-step:
   - **Notion API Key**: notion.so/my-integrations → New integration → Name "Founder OS" → Copy "Internal Integration Secret" (starts with `ntn_`) → Must have "Read content", "Update content", "Insert content" capabilities
   - **Google (gws)**: Run `gws auth login` → Browser opens → Sign in → Approve Gmail + Calendar + Drive scopes → Terminal confirms
   - **Slack Bot Token** (optional): api.slack.com/apps → Create New App → From Manifest → Add bot scopes (`channels:history`, `channels:read`, `users:read`) → Install to Workspace → Copy Bot User OAuth Token (starts with `xoxb-`)
   - **Web Search API Key** (optional): Brief instructions for search provider
4. **Installation** — The 3 commands (clone, configure, run)
5. **What the Installer Does** — Transparent breakdown of all 6 phases
6. **First Commands to Try** — 3-4 example commands to validate end-to-end
7. **Updating** — `git pull && ./install.sh` (idempotent)

### FAQ.md

~15-20 entries including:
- "Do I need all 32 plugins?" — Yes they all install, use what you need
- "Can I use this without Notion?" — Limited, Notion is backbone for 21 plugins
- "What Google permissions does gws need?" — Read-only by default, write for Calendar/Drive features
- "Is my data sent anywhere?" — No, everything local, API calls direct to Notion/Google
- "Can I add custom plugins?" — Yes, follow plugin scaffold template
- "How do I update?" — `git pull && ./install.sh`
- "Can I use with a team?" — Each person runs installer with own API keys
- "What about older Notion databases?" — Migration guide, backward-compatible discovery

### TROUBLESHOOTING.md

Each entry follows Symptom → Cause → Fix → Verify:

- "Notion API key invalid" — Check starts with `ntn_`, integration has capabilities, page shared
- "gws auth fails" — Browser didn't open, wrong account, missing scopes
- "`npx` command not found" — Node.js not installed/not in PATH
- "Database not found" — Run `./install.sh --verify`, check integration access
- "Permission denied on install.sh" — `chmod +x install.sh`
- "Plugin not showing in Claude Code" — Check symlinks, restart Claude Code
- "MCP server connection failed" — Verify `.mcp.json` env vars, check `NOTION_API_KEY` exported
- "gws rate limited" — Wait 60s, retry, reduce `--limit`

## Setup Plugin (founder-os-setup)

A dedicated plugin that provides installation and maintenance commands. Lives alongside the 32 product plugins as plugin #00.

### Structure

```
founder-os-setup/
├── .claude-plugin/
│   └── plugin.json           # Manifest (plugin_number: "00", type: "infrastructure")
├── .mcp.json                 # Notion MCP config
├── commands/
│   ├── setup-notion-hq.md    # /setup:notion-hq — create all HQ databases
│   └── setup-verify.md       # /setup:verify — health check all integrations
├── skills/
│   └── notion-hq-setup/
│       └── SKILL.md          # Database creation logic, schema reference
├── INSTALL.md
└── README.md
```

### `/setup:notion-hq` Command

Invokes the `notion-hq-setup` skill which:
1. Reads `_infrastructure/notion-db-templates/founder-os-hq-manifest.json` for the complete database map (22 databases in `creation_order`)
2. Reads each template JSON by filename from the manifest (handles both `hq-*.json` and legacy-named templates)
3. Creates databases via Notion MCP in dependency order:
   - Companies first (hub for all relations)
   - Then Contacts, Deals, Communications (CRM cluster)
   - Then Tasks, Meetings, Finance (Operations)
   - Then Briefings, Knowledge Base, Research, Reports (Intelligence)
   - Then Content, Deliverables, Prompts (Content)
   - Then Goals, Milestones, Learnings, Weekly Insights, Workflows, Activity Log (Growth)
4. Wires cross-database relations after all databases exist
5. Creates Command Center dashboard page
6. Populates select/multi-select options (status values, type values)

### `/setup:verify` Command

Runs all Phase 6 verification checks from within Claude Code:
- Notion DB connectivity (all 22 databases)
- gws CLI auth status
- Filesystem MCP write/read test
- Reports results with pass/fail per integration

### Idempotency

- Before creating any database, searches for existing `[FOS] [Name]` database
- If found, validates schema matches expected properties
- Reports discrepancies but does not overwrite existing data
- Handles partial state: if 12 of 22 databases exist, creates only the missing 10
- Safe to re-run at any time

## Design Principles

- **Idempotent** — safe to re-run, skips completed steps
- **Fail-fast** — stops at first blocker with clear error + troubleshooting link
- **No manual Notion setup** — `/setup:notion-hq` creates all 22 databases programmatically
- **Secrets never committed** — `.env` in `.gitignore`, `.env.example` committed
- **Updatable** — `git pull && ./install.sh` handles updates
- **Project-scoped** — plugins symlinked to `.claude/plugins/` (project level, not global)
- **Transparent** — user can read exactly what the installer does before running it
- **Clean separation** — dev workflow artifacts never reach end users; release script enforces the boundary

1→# [14] NPM Distribution System Design
     2→
     3→**Date:** 2026-03-15
     4→**Status:** Draft
     5→**Package:** `founder-os` on npm
     6→**Command:** `npx founder-os@latest --init`
     7→
     8→## Problem
     9→
    10→Founder OS is a complete Claude Code automation system. Currently, it has no distribution mechanism — the `founder-os-dist` repo is empty and the dev repo has no npm packaging. Users need a single command to install founderOS into any project directory.
    11→
    12→## Solution
    13→
    14→An npm package called `founder-os` that provides a CLI entry point. Running `npx founder-os@latest --init` copies all founderOS files into the current directory, creating `.founderOS/` (engine/infrastructure) and `.claude/` (commands, skills, agents). The command is idempotent — re-running it updates to the latest version while preserving user customizations.
    15→
    16→## Design Decisions
    17→
    18→| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
    19→|----------|--------|-----------|
    20→| Install mode | Standalone in `.claude/` | Native Claude Code discovery, no plugin system dependency |
    21→| Directory split | `.founderOS/` (engine) + `.claude/` (interface) | Clean separation of infrastructure from Claude Code content |
    22→| Dependency setup | Install-then-guide | Fast install, print next-steps checklist afterward |
    23→| Update strategy | Idempotent re-run of `--init` | Simple mental model, manifest tracks managed files |
    24→| Package name | `founder-os` | Matches repo name, npm-compliant |
    25→| CLI approach | Pure file copy | No framework, no runtime dependencies |
    26→| Plugin system | Bypassed for distribution | See "Plugin System Pivot" section |
    27→
    28→## Plugin System Pivot
    29→
    30→The dev repo uses `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` as a Claude Code plugin. The npm distribution **bypasses the plugin system entirely** and installs files directly into `.claude/`. Rationale:
    31→
    32→1. **No plugin marketplace dependency** — users don't need `claude plugin marketplace add`; `npx` is universal
    33→2. **Simpler mental model** — files are right where Claude Code reads them
    34→3. **No `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` resolution needed** — files reference each other via relative paths from `.claude/`
    35→
    36→### Path Rewriting
    37→
    38→The dev repo uses `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` in command and skill files for portability. During the **release build step**, all occurrences of `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` are rewritten:
    39→
    40→| Dev repo path | Distributed path | Rewrite rule |
    41→|---------------|------------------|--------------|
    42→| `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/skills/...` | `skills/...` | Strip prefix — skills are in `.claude/skills/` |
    43→| `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/commands/...` | `commands/...` | Strip prefix — commands are in `.claude/commands/` |
    44→| `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/agents/...` | `agents/...` | Strip prefix — agents are in `.claude/agents/` |
    45→| `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/_infrastructure/...` | `../../.founderOS/infrastructure/...` | Rewrite to relative path from `.claude/` to `.founderOS/` |
    46→
    47→This rewriting happens in `release.sh` using `sed`, not at install time. The template files in the npm package already have correct paths.
    48→
    49→## Package Structure
    50→
    51→```
    52→founder-os/                     # npm package root (= founder-os-dist repo)
    53→├── package.json                # Full spec below
    54→├── bin/
    55→│   └── founder-os.js           # CLI entry point
    56→├── template/
    57→│   ├── .founderOS/             # Engine files (copied to project)
    58→│   │   ├── version.json        # Written at install time, not bundled
    59→│   │   ├── manifest.json       # Written at install time, not bundled
    60→│   │   ├── config/             # User settings references
    61→│   │   ├── scripts/            # Runtime scripts (see mapping table)
    62→│   │   ├── infrastructure/     # Core infrastructure (see mapping table)
    63→│   │   ├── context/            # Business context storage (empty dir)
    64→│   │   ├── auth/               # Auth state (empty dir)
    65→│   │   └── db/                 # SQLite, memory indexes (empty dir)
    66→│   └── .claude/                # Claude Code interface (copied to project)
    67→│       ├── commands/           # All command namespaces
    68→│       ├── skills/             # All domain skills
    69→│       ├── agents/             # All agent teams
    70→│       ├── settings.json       # Claude Code config with MCP servers
    71→│       └── CLAUDE.md           # Project instructions + best practices
    72→├── CHANGELOG.md
    73→├── LICENSE
    74→└── README.md                   # npm page documentation
    75→```
    76→
    77→### package.json
    78→
    79→```json
    80→{
    81→  "name": "founder-os",
    82→  "version": "1.0.0",
    83→  "description": "AI-powered business automation for founders — installs into any Claude Code project",
    84→  "license": "MIT",
    85→  "author": {
    86→    "name": "Founder OS",
    87→    "email": "contact@founderos.dev"
    88→  },
    89→  "repository": {
    90→    "type": "git",
    91→    "url": "https://github.com/thecloudtips/founder-os-dist.git"
    92→  },
    93→  "keywords": ["claude-code", "ai", "automation", "founder", "business"],
    94→  "bin": {
    95→    "founder-os": "bin/founder-os.js"
    96→  },
    97→  "files": [
    98→    "bin/",
    99→    "template/",
   100→    "README.md",

   102→    "CHANGELOG.md"
   103→  ],
   104→  "engines": {
   105→    "node": ">=18.0.0"
   106→  }
   107→}

   109→
   110→### Template Mapping (Dev Repo → npm Package)
   111→
   112→This table defines exactly what goes from the dev repo into `template/`:
   113→
   114→| Dev repo source | Template destination | Notes |
   115→|-----------------|---------------------|-------|
   116→| `commands/` | `template/.claude/commands/` | All command namespaces |
   117→| `skills/` | `template/.claude/skills/` | All domain skills |
   118→| `agents/` | `template/.claude/agents/` | All agent team configs |

   121→| `_infrastructure/preflight/` | `template/.founderOS/infrastructure/preflight/` | Dependency validation |
   122→| `_infrastructure/intelligence/` | `template/.founderOS/infrastructure/intelligence/` | Adaptive intelligence |

   127→| `_infrastructure/scout/` | `template/.founderOS/infrastructure/scout/` | Tool discovery |

   133→| `scripts/package.json` | `template/.founderOS/scripts/package.json` | Script dependencies |
   134→
   135→**Excluded from distribution (dev-only):**
   136→
   137→| Dev repo path | Reason |
   138→|---------------|--------|
   139→| `_infrastructure/notion-db-templates/` | Used by setup command, references templates by content not file |
   140→| `_infrastructure/notion-hq/` | Installation guides consumed during setup, not at runtime |
   141→| `_infrastructure/automation-audit/` | Dev verification tool |

   146→| `docs/` | Dev documentation |
   147→| `.claude-plugin/` | Plugin manifest not used in standalone install |
   148→
   149→## CLI Behavior
   150→
   151→### Commands
   152→
   153→| Flag | Behavior |
   154→|------|----------|
   155→| `--init` | Install or update founderOS (main command) |
   156→| `--version` | Print package version and installed version (if any) |
   157→| `--status` | Show what's installed, what's outdated, what's modified |
   158→| `--uninstall` | Remove all founderOS-managed files (respects manifest) |
   159→| `--force` | Modifier for `--init` or `--uninstall`: overwrite modified files (creates `.bak` backups) |
   160→
   161→### Fresh Install Flow
   162→
   163→1. Check Node.js version >= 18; exit with error if not met
   164→2. Check write permissions on current directory; exit with error if denied
   165→3. Check if `.founderOS/version.json` exists — if yes, redirect to Update Flow
   166→4. Create `.founderOS/` directory
   167→5. Copy `template/.founderOS/` contents → `./.founderOS/`
   168→6. Create `.claude/` directory if it doesn't exist
   169→7. For each file in `template/.claude/`:
   170→   - If file already exists at destination (pre-existing user file with same path):
   171→     - **Back it up** to `<filename>.pre-founderos.bak`
   172→     - Copy founderOS version
   173→     - Record in manifest with status `"replaced-existing"` and backup path
   174→   - If file doesn't exist: copy it, record in manifest as `"managed"`
   175→8. Handle CLAUDE.md specially (see CLAUDE.md Generation section)
   176→9. Handle settings.json specially (see settings.json Merge section)
   177→10. Write `.founderOS/version.json` with version, timestamp
   178→11. Write `.founderOS/manifest.json` with all file checksums
   179→12. Print success + next steps
   180→
   181→**Note on step 7:** Pre-existing files at founderOS paths are rare (a user would have to manually create `commands/inbox/triage.md` before installing). The backup preserves their work. On `--uninstall`, backed-up files are restored.
   182→
   183→### Update Flow
   184→
   185→1. Read `.founderOS/version.json` → get installed version
   186→2. Compare with package version:
   187→   - Same version without `--force`: print "Already up to date. Use --force to repair." and exit
   188→   - Same version with `--force`: proceed (repair mode)
   189→   - Different version: proceed (update mode)
   190→3. Read `.founderOS/manifest.json`
   191→4. For each file in the manifest:
   192→   - Compute SHA-256 of file on disk
   193→   - If file missing: mark as `"user-removed"` in manifest, don't recreate
   194→   - If checksum matches manifest (unchanged): overwrite with new version, update checksum
   195→   - If checksum differs (user-modified):
   196→     - Without `--force`: skip, add to warnings list
   197→     - With `--force`: create `.bak` backup, overwrite, update checksum
   198→5. For files in new version but not in manifest (new files): copy, add to manifest
   199→6. For files in manifest but not in new version (removed files):
   200→   - If checksum matches (unmodified): delete file, remove from manifest
   201→   - If checksum differs (user-modified): keep file, remove from manifest (becomes user's file)
   202→7. Update `.founderOS/infrastructure/` wholesale (engine files are not user-modified)
   203→8. Handle CLAUDE.md merge (preserve user content outside markers)
   204→9. Handle settings.json merge (deep merge)
   205→10. Update `version.json` and `manifest.json`
   206→11. Print update summary
   207→
   208→### Interrupted Install Recovery
   209→
   210→The manifest is written **last** (step 11 of fresh install). If installation is interrupted:
   211→
   212→- `.founderOS/` exists but `manifest.json` is missing
   213→- Next `--init` run detects this state: `.founderOS/` exists but no `version.json` or `manifest.json`
   214→- Treats this as a fresh install: re-copies all files, generates manifest from scratch
   215→- Any partially-copied files are overwritten cleanly
   216→
   217→### Output Examples
   218→
   219→**Fresh install:**
   220→```
   221→$ npx founder-os@latest --init
   222→
   223→  Founder OS v1.0.0
   224→
   225→  ✓ Created .founderOS/
   226→  ✓ Created .claude/commands/ (N namespaces)
   227→  ✓ Created .claude/skills/ (N domains)
   228→  ✓ Created .claude/agents/ (N teams)
   229→  ✓ Generated .claude/CLAUDE.md
   230→  ✓ Generated .claude/settings.json
   231→
   232→  Done! Next steps:
   233→
   234→    1. Set NOTION_API_KEY in your environment
   235→    2. Run `gws auth login` for Gmail/Calendar/Drive
   236→    3. Open Claude Code and try:
   237→       /inbox triage    — Process your inbox
   238→       /briefing        — Get your daily briefing
   239→       /prep            — Prepare for meetings
   240→```
   241→
   242→Counts (N) are computed dynamically by counting directories/files, never hardcoded.
   243→
   244→**Update:**
   245→```
   246→$ npx founder-os@latest --init
   247→
   248→  Founder OS v1.0.0 → v1.3.0
   249→
   250→  ✓ Updated 12 commands
   251→  ✓ Updated 8 skills
   252→  ✓ Updated 2 agent teams
   253→  ✓ Refreshed .founderOS/ infrastructure
   254→  ⚠ Skipped 3 files (modified by you):
   255→    .claude/commands/inbox/triage.md
   256→    .claude/skills/inbox/SKILL.md
   257→    .claude/CLAUDE.md
   258→
   259→  Done! See CHANGELOG for what's new.
   260→```
   261→
   262→## CLAUDE.md Generation
   263→
   264→The generated CLAUDE.md uses section markers to separate founderOS-managed content from user content:
   265→
   266→```markdown
   267→<!-- founder-os:start -->
   268→# Founder OS
   269→
   270→AI-powered business automation for founders.
   271→
   272→## Quick Start
   273→
   274→- `/inbox triage` — Process and prioritize your inbox
   275→- `/briefing` — Get your daily briefing
   276→- `/prep` — Prepare for upcoming meetings
   277→- `/report weekly` — Generate weekly status report
   278→
   279→## Command Reference
   280→
   281→### Daily Operations
   282→| Command | Description |
   283→|---------|-------------|
   284→| `/inbox triage` | Prioritize emails, draft responses |
   285→| `/briefing` | Calendar + email + Slack digest |
   286→| `/prep` | Meeting preparation with context |
   287→| `/review` | End-of-day review and follow-ups |
   288→| `/followup` | Track and send follow-ups |
   289→| `/meeting` | Meeting notes and action items |
   290→
   291→### Business Documents
   292→| Command | Description |
   293→|---------|-------------|
   294→| `/report weekly` | Status reports from your data |
   295→| `/invoice create` | Generate invoices |
   296→| `/proposal draft` | Write business proposals |
   297→| `/contract` | Contract drafting |
   298→| `/sow` | Statements of work |
   299→| `/expense` | Expense tracking |
   300→
   301→### Communications
   302→| Command | Description |
   303→|---------|-------------|
   304→| `/newsletter draft` | Newsletter composition |
   305→| `/linkedin post` | LinkedIn content |
   306→| `/social cross-post` | Multi-platform publishing |
   307→
   308→### Integrations
   309→| Command | Description |
   310→|---------|-------------|
   311→| `/notion` | Notion workspace operations |
   312→| `/slack` | Slack channel operations |
   313→| `/drive` | Google Drive management |
   314→| `/crm` | CRM operations |
   315→
   316→### Intelligence & Meta
   317→| Command | Description |
   318→|---------|-------------|
   319→| `/memory` | Cross-session memory |
   320→| `/intel health` | Intelligence engine status |
   321→| `/scout find` | Discover new tools and skills |
   322→| `/workflow` | Automation workflows |
   323→
   324→## Configuration
   325→
   326→### Required
   327→- `NOTION_API_KEY` — Your Notion integration token
   328→- `gws auth login` — Google Workspace CLI authentication
   329→
   330→### Optional
   331→- Slack MCP server for `/slack` commands
   332→- Late.dev account for social media publishing
   333→
   334→## Architecture
   335→
   336→- **Commands** (`.claude/commands/`) — WHAT to do (YAML frontmatter specs)
   337→- **Skills** (`.claude/skills/`) — HOW to do it (domain knowledge)
   338→- **Agents** (`.claude/agents/`) — WHO does it (team coordination)
   339→- **Infrastructure** (`.founderOS/`) — Shared services (dispatcher, memory, intelligence)
   340→
   341→## Customization
   342→
   343→Add your own commands to `.claude/commands/` — they won't be overwritten
   344→on update. To override a founderOS command, create a file with the same
   345→path and name; your version takes precedence.
   346→
   347→## Updating
   348→
   349→```bash
   350→npx founder-os@latest --init
   351→```
   352→
   353→Re-run anytime. Your customizations are preserved.
   354→<!-- founder-os:end -->
   355→```
   356→
   357→### Merge Behavior
   358→
   359→| Scenario | Behavior |
   360→|----------|----------|
   361→| No existing CLAUDE.md | Create new file with founderOS section only |
   362→| Existing CLAUDE.md without markers | Prepend founderOS section with markers, blank line, then existing content |
   363→| Existing CLAUDE.md with markers | Replace content between markers only, preserve everything outside |
   364→
   365→### Uninstall Behavior
   366→
   367→On `--uninstall`, the CLI removes **only the content between markers** from CLAUDE.md. If the file becomes empty after removal, delete it. Otherwise, leave the user's content intact.
   368→
   369→## settings.json Merge Strategy
   370→
   371→The CLI generates this base `settings.json` for founderOS:
   372→
   373→```json
   374→{
   375→  "permissions": {
   376→    "allow": [
   377→      "Bash(npx founder-os*)",
   378→      "Bash(node .founderOS/scripts/*)"
   379→    ]
   380→  }
   381→}
   382→```
   383→
   384→### Merge Rules
   385→
   386→1. Read existing `.claude/settings.json` (if any)
   387→2. For `permissions.allow`: append founderOS entries if not already present
   388→3. Never remove existing user entries
   389→4. Write merged result
   390→5. On `--uninstall`: remove only founderOS-added entries from `permissions.allow`
   391→
   392→MCP server configuration is **not** added to `settings.json` by the CLI. MCP servers require user-specific credentials (API keys, auth tokens) and are configured separately via the next-steps guide or `/setup` commands.
   393→
   394→## Manifest Format
   395→
   396→`.founderOS/manifest.json` tracks all managed files:
   397→
   398→```json
   399→{
   400→  "version": "1.3.0",
   401→  "installedAt": "2026-03-15T12:00:00Z",
   402→  "updatedAt": "2026-03-15T12:00:00Z",
   403→  "files": {
   404→    ".claude/commands/inbox/triage.md": {
   405→      "checksum": "sha256:abc123def456...",
   406→      "status": "managed"
   407→    },
   408→    ".claude/commands/inbox/drafts-approved.md": {
   409→      "checksum": "sha256:789abc...",
   410→      "status": "user-modified"
   411→    },
   412→    ".claude/commands/custom/my-command.md": {
   413→      "status": "user-removed"
   414→    },
   415→    ".claude/commands/report/generate.md": {
   416→      "checksum": "sha256:...",
   417→      "status": "replaced-existing",
   418→      "backup": ".claude/commands/report/generate.md.pre-founderos.bak"
   419→    }
   420→  }
   421→}
   422→```
   423→
   424→**Status values:**
   425→- `managed` — founderOS-owned, safe to overwrite on update
   426→- `user-modified` — checksum differs from install, skip on update
   427→- `user-removed` — user deleted this file, don't recreate
   428→- `replaced-existing` — file existed before founderOS, backup created
   429→
   430→## Edge Cases
   431→
   432→| Scenario | Behavior |
   433→|----------|----------|
   434→| No `.claude/` directory | Create it |
   435→| `.claude/` exists with user commands | founderOS files alongside, never overwrite user files in different paths |
   436→| Pre-existing file at a founderOS path | Back up to `.pre-founderos.bak`, install founderOS version |
   437→| `.claude/CLAUDE.md` exists without markers | Prepend founderOS section with markers |
   438→| `.claude/CLAUDE.md` exists with markers | Replace between markers only |
   439→| `.claude/settings.json` exists | Merge permissions, don't overwrite |
   440→| User deleted a founderOS command | Don't recreate on update (tracked as `user-removed`) |
   441→| User modified a founderOS command | Skip on update (warn), or overwrite with `--force` (backup created) |
   442→| No Node.js >= 18 | Exit with: "Founder OS requires Node.js 18+. Current: [version]" |
   443→| No write permissions | Exit with: "Cannot write to [directory]. Check permissions." |
   444→| npm registry unreachable | npx handles this natively (network error) |
   445→| Interrupted install (no manifest) | Next `--init` detects missing manifest, re-runs fresh install |
   446→| `--uninstall` with modifications | Without `--force`: list modified files, skip them. With `--force`: backup and remove. |
   447→| `--uninstall` restores backups | Files with `replaced-existing` status: restore from `.pre-founderos.bak` |
   448→| `.founderOS/` exists but no `version.json` | Treat as corrupted/partial install, re-run fresh |
   449→
   450→## Release Pipeline
   451→
   452→### Build Step (from dev repo)
   453→
   454→The existing `scripts/release.sh` is **replaced** with a new version that:
   455→
   456→1. Runs from the `founderOS/` dev repo root
   457→2. Creates a clean `build/` directory
   458→3. Assembles the template (see Template Mapping table above):
   459→   - Copies included directories into `build/template/.claude/` and `build/template/.founderOS/`
   460→   - Excludes dev-only directories (see exclusion table above)
   461→4. Rewrites `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` paths in all `.md` files under `build/template/.claude/`:
   462→   - `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/skills/` → `skills/`
   463→   - `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/commands/` → `commands/`
   464→   - `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/agents/` → `agents/`
   465→   - `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/_infrastructure/` → `../../.founderOS/infrastructure/`
   466→5. Generates `build/template/.claude/CLAUDE.md` from template content
   467→6. Generates `build/template/.claude/settings.json` with base config
   468→7. Copies `bin/founder-os.js` to `build/bin/`
   469→8. Copies `package.json`, `README.md`, `LICENSE`, `CHANGELOG.md` to `build/`
   470→9. Bumps version in `build/package.json`
   471→10. Syncs `build/` contents → `founder-os-dist/` repo (rsync --delete)
   472→11. Commits and tags in `founder-os-dist`
   473→12. Runs `npm publish` from `founder-os-dist/`
   474→
   475→### founder-os-dist Repo
   476→
   477→The `founder-os-dist` repository **is** the npm package source. It contains exactly what gets published to npm. The repo structure after a release matches the Package Structure section above. This replaces the previously planned marketplace structure.
   478→
   479→### Version Strategy
   480→
   481→- Semantic versioning: `MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH`
   482→- MAJOR: Breaking changes (restructured directories, removed commands)
   483→- MINOR: New commands, skills, or features
   484→- PATCH: Bug fixes, documentation updates
   485→- `package.json` version is the source of truth
   486→- `.founderOS/version.json` (at install site) tracks the installed version
   487→
   488→## CLI Implementation Notes
   489→
   490→The CLI (`bin/founder-os.js`) uses only Node.js built-in modules:
   491→
   492→- `fs` — file operations (synchronous for simplicity)
   493→- `path` — path manipulation
   494→- `crypto` — SHA-256 checksums
   495→- `child_process` — none needed
   496→
   497→**Argument parsing:** Manual `process.argv` parsing (no library). The flag set is small and fixed.
   498→
   499→**Expected size:** 400-500 lines. The CLAUDE.md merge logic, settings.json merge, manifest management, and backup/restore logic are non-trivial.
   500→
   501→**Exit codes:**
   502→- `0` — success
   503→- `1` — error (permissions, Node version, corrupted state)
   504→
   505→## Technical Constraints
   506→
   507→- **Node.js >= 18** required (for native `fs.cpSync`, `crypto.createHash`)
   508→- **No runtime dependencies** — only Node.js built-ins
   509→- **No build step for the CLI** — plain JavaScript
   510→- **File operations are synchronous** — simpler code, file count is manageable
   511→- **Checksums use SHA-256** via `crypto.createHash`
   512→- **macOS and Linux only** — Claude Code doesn't support Windows
   513→
   514→## Out of Scope
   515→
   516→- Interactive setup wizard (users configure integrations separately)
   517→- Plugin marketplace integration (separate concern from npm distribution)
   518→- Auto-update mechanism (users explicitly re-run `--init`)
   519→- Telemetry or analytics
   520→- MCP server auto-configuration (requires user credentials)
   521→- Hooks directory in `.claude/` (founderOS does not currently ship hook files; hooks are defined in settings.json or added by users)
   522→
   523→## Success Criteria
   524→
   525→1. `npx founder-os@latest --init` installs successfully in under 5 seconds (goal, not hard requirement)
   526→2. All command namespaces are accessible via `/command` in Claude Code after install
   527→3. Re-running `--init` preserves all user modifications (verified by checksum comparison)
   528→4. `--uninstall` cleanly removes all founderOS files without affecting user files
   529→5. Pre-existing files backed up during install are restored on `--uninstall`
   530→6. The generated CLAUDE.md teaches a new user how to use founderOS effectively
   531→7. Release pipeline produces a working npm package from the dev repo in one script run
   532→8. `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` references are fully rewritten — no broken paths in distributed files
   533→
